title: China-led digital payments system mBridge set for commercial launch
text: A China-led blockchain-based cross-border currency platform is set for its commercial launch, according to the Financial Times.

text: Waniwani, a fintech startup that uses AI to helps vendors surface their technology to financial institutions, has raised an $8 million Seed round.

title: Anthropic shutdown shines spotlight on EU tech sovereignty push
text: The US government has ordered Anthropic to cut foreign nationals off from its two most powerful models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, reinforcing European concerns about reliance on US-imported technology.

title: ASX settles legal case over bungled Chess project
text: The Australian Securities Exchange (ASX) is to pay a $20.5 million penalty to settle a legal case brought by the Australian Securities and Investment Commission (Asic), which alleged that the stock market operator deliberately misled the market about the progress of its post-trade Chess replacement project.

title: APP fraud back on the rise as crooks stole £1.3bn in 2025 - UK Finance
text: Criminals stole £1.28 billion through fraud in the UK last year, up four per cent on 2024, with authorised push payment (APP) fraud rising sharply following a drop off the previous year.

title: Current raises $80m
text: Current has raised $80 million in a Series E equity financing round that values the US personal finance app at $1.5 billion.

title: Appeals court upholds Bankman-Fried's conviction
text: A US federal appeals court has upheld FTX co-founder Sam Bankman-Fried's conviction, finding that the trial that led to his 25-year prison term was not unfair.

title: Zelle heads to India; preps stablecoin for global expansion
text: US bank-owned P2P payments service Zelle is going global, with users soon getting the ability to send money to friends and family in India.
